research.interface: Schema Registry REST API, media type application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json (Confluent Platform, current docs)

official_sources.title: Schema Registry API Reference for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: media types (application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json preferred, +json/application/json fallbacks); POST /subjects/{subject}/versions request fields schema/schemaType/references with reference objects of {name, subject, version}; POST /subjects/{subject} lookup request/response (subject, id, version, schema) and 40403; POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/{version} with 'latest', verbose=true and is_compatible/messages; error codes 40401, 40402, 42201, 42202

official_sources.title: Schema Registry API Usage Examples for Confluent Platform
official_sources.used_for: exact register/lookup/compatibility request examples with Content-Type application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json; register response carrying the schema id; lookup response {subject, version, id, schema}; {"is_compatible": true} compatibility response; the rule that the response includes schemaType for JSON Schema and Protobuf but omits it for the default AVRO

official_sources.title: Release Notes for Confluent Platform 7.7
official_sources.used_for: documented 429 rate-limit behavior: 'Schema Registry clients built with Confluent Platform 7.7.4 and later include auto retries for 429 exceptions' — the basis for this client's bounded 429 retry policy

research.language_rationale: Confluent's official Schema Registry client is the Java library (io.confluent:kafka-schema-registry-client), so Java is the native language for registry tooling; this release gate speaks the documented REST surface directly with java.net.http so the CI image needs no Confluent packages, while staying byte-compatible with the documented v1 media type contract.

verified_facts: Schema Registry requests and responses use application/vnd.schemaregistry.v1+json; application/vnd.schemaregistry+json and application/json are accepted fallbacks.

POST /subjects/{subject}/versions registers a schema; the body carries schema, optional schemaType (AVRO default, JSON and PROTOBUF explicit) and optional references; the response returns the globally unique schema id.
Each schema reference is an object of name (the reference name used inside the schema), subject (where the referenced schema is registered) and version (the referenced version).

POST /subjects/{subject} checks whether a schema is already registered under the subject, returning subject/id/version/schema on a hit, 40403 when the schema is absent and 40401 when the subject does not exist.

POST /compatibility/subjects/{subject}/versions/latest tests a candidate against the last registered version; with ?verbose=true the response carries is_compatible plus messages explaining failures; valid version segments are 1..2^31-1, 'latest', or -1 meaning latest.
Error responses use the {error_code, message} envelope with codes such as 40401 (subject not found), 40402 (version not found), 42201 (invalid schema) and 42202 (invalid version).

Schema Registry clients built with Confluent Platform 7.7.4 and later auto-retry 429 rate-limit responses, so a well-behaved REST client must back off and retry 429s without producing duplicate registrations.
